2603.15297.title: Evolutionary Transfer Learning for Dragonchess
2603.15297.paper_summary: Dragonchess, a three-dimensional chess variant introduced by Gary Gygax, presents unique strategic and computational challenges that make it an ideal environment for studying the transfer of artificial intelligence (AI) heuristics across domains. In this work, we introduce Dragonchess as a novel testbed for AI research and provide an open-source, Python-based game engine for community use. Our research investigates evolutionary transfer learning by adapting heuristic evaluation functions directly from Stockfish, a leading chess engine, and subsequently optimizing them using Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy (CMA-ES). Initial trials showed that direct heuristic transfers were inadequate due to Dragonchess's distinct multi-layer structure and movement rules. However, evolutionary optimization significantly improved AI agent performance, resulting in superior gameplay demonstrated through empirical evaluation in a 50-round Swiss-style tournament. This research establishes the effectiveness of evolutionary methods in adapting heuristic knowledge to structurally complex, previously unexplored game domains.
2603.15297.paper_summary_zh: Dragonchess，由Gary Gygax引入的三維棋類變體，提出了獨特的策略和計算挑戰，使其成為研究人工智慧（AI）啟發式方法跨領域轉移的理想環境。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們將Dragonchess介紹為一個新穎的AI研究測試平台，並提供一個基於Python的開源遊戲引擎供社群使用。<lb/>我們的研究通過直接從領先的棋類引擎Stockfish調整啟發式評估函數來調查進化轉移學習，並隨後使用協方差矩陣適應進化策略（CMA-ES）進行優化。<lb/>初步試驗顯示，由於Dragonchess獨特的多層結構和移動規則，直接的啟發式轉移是不足的。<lb/>然而，進化優化顯著提高了AI代理的性能，通過在50輪瑞士式錦標賽中的實證評估展示了卓越的遊戲表現。<lb/>這項研究確立了進化方法在將啟發式知識適應於結構複雜、先前未探索的遊戲領域中的有效性。
2603.15297.author: Jim O'Connor et.al.
2603.15297.authors: Jim O'Connor, Annika Hoag, Sarah Goyette, Gary B. Parker
